Patch Set 1:

After deep think about this design, I think it has some problem for LMV overstepping... If two stripes on a same MDT, then the trash stripe will have the same name, that is not correct...
We should still use the stripe shard FID as the name of stub dir, I think.

Patch Set 1:

> After deep think about this design, I think it has some problem for
 > LMV overstepping... If two stripes on a same MDT, then the trash
 > stripe will have the same name, that is not correct...
 > We should still use the stripe shard FID as the name of stub dir, I
 > think.

Hmm, I'd hate to make things worse in the Trash Can for a feature that is mostly only used for benchmarking (or rarely maybe some crazy billion-file directory). 

How will TC know these shards are part of the same striped directory when the master directory is deleted?  I guess that TC could look up the shard FIDs and replace those in the master directory, or create a striped stub master directory at the beginning (when first file of striped directory is deleted) so that the mechanism for handling parent directory is unchanged?

Patch Set 1:

We known the shard FID, we can use this FID as the name of stub dir (Trash shard). When deleting the striped dir, we can create a new striped dir on Trash Can use the stub dirs as Trash shards.

Patch Set 1:

Maybe you are right, we should create a striped stub master directory at the beginning of the first file of striped directory is deleted, but with the name of trash shard with the FID str of the corresponding shard of the striped dir
Topic set to tcu

parents.subject: LU-18958 tcu: add NOTRASH flag for files and directories

commit.subject: LU-18985 tcu: use master pfid as stub dir name for striped dir
commit.message:
LU-18985 tcu: use master pfid as stub dir name for striped dir

When files in a striped directory are deleted, it should use
master parent FID as the stub dir name in Trash Can (instead
of the stripe shard FID).

Add sanityn/test_117o to verify it.

Signed-off-by: Yingjin Qian <qian@ddn.com>
Change-Id: I82238b949fd1b2556f0624f5a92f67741ea85368
